text: Continuous integration is a software development practice where developers frequently merge their code changes into a shared main repository. Each time code is pushed, automated builds are triggered. These CI pipelines then run tests, such as unit and integration tests, to check for errors. The main benefit is catching issues early, often just minutes after the code is added. This allows developers to fix small problems before they become larger, more costly bugs. Using continuous integration helps teams maintain a stable codebase, work together more efficiently, and release higher-quality software with greater confidence.
